Title:

When Small Variations Become Big Failures: Reliability Challenges in Compute-in-Memory Neural Accelerators

Authors:

Yifan Qin

View a PDF of the paper titled When Small Variations Become Big Failures: Reliability Challenges in Compute-in-Memory Neural Accelerators, by Yifan Qin and 5 other authors

View PDF

HTML (experimental)

Abstract:

Compute-in-memory (CiM) architectures promise significant improvements in energy efficiency and throughput for deep neural network acceleration by alleviating the von Neumann bottleneck. However, their reliance on emerging non-volatile memory devices introduces device-level non-idealities-such as write variability, conductance drift, and stochastic noise-that fundamentally challenge reliability, predictability, and safety, especially in safety-critical applications. This talk examines

results.title: Attention Residuals
results.document:
[chunk 0]
Title: Attention Residuals

### 한줄 요약
Transformer의 고정 residual accumulation을 depth-wise softmax attention으로 대체하는 Attention Residuals와 Block AttnRes를 제안한 Kimi Team 기술 보고서다.

### 핵심 기여
- PreNorm residual path에서 깊이에 따라 hidden-state magnitude가 커지고 레이어 기여가 희석되는 문제를 정식화한다.
- 각 레이어가 이전 레이어 출력 전체를 선택적으로 집계하는 Attention Residuals를 제안한다.
- 대규모 학습 비용을 줄이기 위해 블록 대표 표현에만 attention하는 Block AttnRes를 설계한다.
- Kimi Linear 48B/3B activated 설정에서 reasoning, code, Chinese benchmark 개선을 보고한다.

### 방법론
Residual sum 대신 이전 depth representation에 대한 softmax attention을 사용해 content-dependent aggregation을 수행한다.

### 한계
arXiv 논문이 아니라 GitHub technical report 공개본 기준이며, 재현 코드/체크포인트보다는 설계와 결과 보고 중심이다.

Title:

LightMem: Lightweight and Efficient Memory-Augmented Generation

Authors:

Yuqi Tang

Shumin Deng

View a PDF of the paper titled LightMem: Lightweight and Efficient Memory-Augmented Generation, by Jizhan Fang and 11 other authors

View PDF

HTML (experimental)

Abstract:

Despite their remarkable capabilities, Large Language Models (LLMs) struggle to effectively leverage historical interaction information in dynamic and complex environments. Memory systems enable LLMs to move beyond stateless interactions by introducing persistent information storage, retrieval, and utilization mechanisms. results.document:
[chunk 2]
Title: [2603.01761] Modular Memory is the Key to Continual Learning Agents
URL: https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.01761
Section: 

the strengths of In-Weight Learning (IWL) and the newly emerged capabilities of In-Context Learning (ICL) through the design of modular memory is the missing piece for continual adaptation at scale. We outline a conceptual framework for modular memory-centric architectures that leverage ICL for rapid adaptation and knowledge accumulation, and IWL for stable updates to model capabilities, charting a practical roadmap toward continually learning agents.

Comments:

This work stems from discussions held at the Dagstuhl seminar on Continual Learning in the Era of Foundation Models (October 2025)

Machine Learning (cs.LG)

; Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI)
